_note: Cascade retrofit of lighting-layout/examples/uk-undersized-lighting-vs-target. 12 LED_PANEL_600 (3500 lm value-engineered substitution) in 8×6 m private office. Upstream lumen-method already FAILS (434 < 500). Photometric verification confirms achieved_min 275 lux < 350 (0.7×500) → INV-01 FAIL HIGH. task_area_compliant=false propagates upstream to lighting-layout INV-11 with cascaded critical flag. Failure-mode demo end-to-end.

consumed_summary._layout_note: 12 panels in 3×4 grid: x ∈ {1000, 3000, 5000, 7000} × y ∈ {1000, 3000, 5000}. The value-engineered 3500 lm panel (vs 4500 lm ontology default) was specified without a lumen-method re-check; upstream INV-1 already FAILS. Photometric-analysis confirms the failure cascades to per-point: cold-corner min 275 lux < 350 threshold.

ies_files._source: Synthetic 600x600mm recessed LED panel, value-engineered 3500lm variant (vs 4500lm ontology default), 4000K, opal diffuser. Distribution: Lambertian cos(theta) per CIBSE LG7 §6.2. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — used to demonstrate the per-point failure cascade for under-spec'd luminaire substitutions.

grid_metadata.grid_spacing_formula: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §6.2 adaptive: p = 0.2 × 5^log₁₀(d) clamped [50, 1000] mm; for d=7.0m task area: p ≈ 0.2 × 5^0.845 × 1000 ≈ 779 mm → snapped to nominal 800 mm. Grid fitted to task area: 10 cols × 7 rows = 70 points (x-step ~778 mm to fit 7000mm extent across 9 intervals; y-step ~833 mm to fit 5000mm extent across 6 intervals).

reflectances._source: CIBSE LG7 §6.2 clean office reflectances (0.7/0.5/0.2) — matches lighting-layout assumptions.

non_compliance_flags.message: Achieved min illuminance 275 lux at NW corner (500, 5500) FAILS BS EN 12464-1 §4.4: per-point min must be ≥ 0.7 × target 500 = 350 lux. Gap 75 lux (21% under threshold). Root cause: value-engineered 3500 lm panel substitution from 4500 lm ontology default reduces total room lumens by 22%; the cold-corner sees the full reduction in addition to the wall-shadow effect. Remediation (matches upstream lumen-method advice): bump to 16 panels in 4×4 grid OR upgrade to 4500 lm panels and keep the 12-fitting layout.
non_compliance_flags.reference: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 per-point requirement + Table 5.3 row 5.34 private_office

non_compliance_flags.message: Achieved avg illuminance 434 lux FAILS the 500 lux target (cross-check: matches upstream lumen-method achieved_illuminance_lux=434.0). This skill does NOT independently flag avg-below-target — that's the lighting-layout INV-1 flag, which is already raised upstream. Carried here for engineer visibility in the cascade walkthrough.
non_compliance_flags.reference: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 illuminance criterion (informational — upstream lighting-layout INV-1 owns this flag)

rationale.chat_summary: FAILURE-MODE CASCADE DEMO. lighting-layout uk-undersized already FAILED upstream lumen-method (avg 434 < 500). Photometric-analysis confirms the failure cascades to per-point: achieved_min 275 lux < 350 (0.7×500) → INV-01 FAIL HIGH. U₀ 0.63 ≥ 0.6 → INV-02 PASS (uniformity itself is OK; the layout is uniformly under-bright). UGR 17.4 ≤ 19 → INV-03 PASS. Intent payload sets task_area_compliant=false and propagates 1 critical non_compliance_flag upstream to lighting-layout INV-11 via the _cascaded_from attribution mechanism (post-D.2 retrofit).

sections.title: Cascade context — failure-mode end-to-end
sections.summary: Consumes electrical/lighting-layout/examples/uk-undersized-lighting-vs-target/intent-out.json. The upstream is the canonical 'value-engineered substitution that broke lumen-method' example — the engineer substituted 3500 lm panels for 4500 lm panels without re-running the lumen calc, and the upstream INV-1 caught it. This cascade confirms the failure also manifests as a per-point BS EN 12464-1 §4.4 violation, demonstrating that the upstream + downstream verification layers agree on the failure diagnosis.

sections.title: Grid resolution derivation
sections.summary: Task area 7×5 m after 500mm border. d=7m; corrected §6.2 formula p ≈ 779 mm snapped to 800 mm per [grid-spacing-rules#adaptive-formula]. 10×7 = 70 grid points (x-step ~778 mm to fit 7000mm, y-step ~833 mm to fit 5000mm extent).

sections.title: Per-point illuminance — the failure
sections.summary: achieved_avg 434 lux (matches upstream lumen-method exactly). achieved_min 275 lux at NW corner (500, 5500); 75 lux below the 350 threshold (21% deficit). The cold-corner deficit is the combination of (a) overall room under-provision (22% lumen reduction from the substitution) plus (b) wall-shadow effect at the perimeter. achieved_max 552 lux at room centre directly under the central panel cluster. U₀ 0.63 is acceptable — the failure is in absolute level not in spatial distribution.

sections.title: UGR + uniformity PASS — INDEPENDENCE demonstrated
sections.summary: Even though INV-01 (per-point illuminance) FAILS, INV-02 (uniformity) and INV-03 (UGR) PASS. Lesson: under-provision is NOT a uniformity problem — the layout is uniformly under-bright, not unevenly bright. The 0.63 U₀ matches the geometry of a 3×4 uniform panel grid (typical 0.7-0.8 U₀); the UGR 17.4 ≤ 19 limit is similarly geometry-driven (luminance-per-fixture × area-projection toward eye) and unaffected by total lumen reduction.

sections.title: Cascade signal — propagation
sections.summary: Intent payload sets task_area_compliant=false AND non_compliance_flags carries 1 critical entry (INV-01 cold-corner). When lighting-layout INV-11 reads this (post-D.2 retrofit): (1) consumed_intents.photometric_grid present ✓; (2) payload.task_area_compliant == false → INV-11 FAIL HIGH; (3) the critical flag is mirrored into lighting-layout's own non_compliance_flags with _cascaded_from='photometric-analysis' attribution. Engineer-of-record sees both the upstream INV-1 (lumen-method) flag AND the cascaded INV-11 flag — confirming the diagnosis from two independent verification paths.

sections.title: Honest disclosure — synthetic IES + remediation
sections.summary: IES file LED_PANEL_600-3500lm.ies is synthetic_reference_C3 — the same Lambertian distribution as the 4500 lm reference but scaled to 3500 lm output. Real value-engineered panels may have different distributions (sometimes tighter beam at the lower lumen output, which would worsen the cold-corner deficit further). Engineer-of-record substitutes project IES before final design freeze. Remediation per upstream lumen-method flag: bump to 16 panels in 4×4 grid (achieved 579 lux per upstream) OR upgrade to 4500 lm and keep the 12-fitting layout (achieved 558 lux per upstream); either gives photometric INV-01 PASS on re-run.

invariants.evidence: Achieved min 275 lux at NW corner (500, 5500) < 0.7 × target 500 = 350 lux. FAIL by 75 lux (21% deficit). Per BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 + Table 5.3 row 5.34 private_office. Cross-check: upstream lumen-method INV-1 also FAILs (avg 434 < 500). Two-layer verification agrees the layout is under-provisioned.

Achieved U₀ = 275/434 = 0.634 ≥ 0.6 private_office target per [ugr-rules#per-room-type-limits]. The 3×4 uniform grid produces acceptable uniformity — the under-provision is in absolute level, not spatial distribution. PASS by 0.034 margin (slim but valid).

Max UGR 17.4 (auto_S_wall observer at (4000, 1000)) ≤ 19 private_office limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34. UGR depends on per-luminaire luminance × geometry, not on total room lumens; the substitution to 3500 lm doesn't affect UGR (each panel is still well below glare threshold from observer angles). 1.6 margin.

1 distinct luminaire_type (LED_PANEL_600) in upstream lighting-layout consumed_summary; 1 ies_files[] entry matches; LM-63 parser returns total_lumens=3500 (the value-engineered figure, distinct from the 4500 lm 4500lm.ies file used in other cascades).
grid_spacing_mm=800 matches BS EN 12464-1 §6.2 formula output p ≈ 779 mm within ±50 mm tolerance. d=7.0m task area.
illuminance_grid.length (full runtime emission) = point_count=70. 10-sample slice in this IR; every (x_mm, y_mm) inside task_area_bounds [500,7500]×[500,5500]. No duplicate coordinates.
4 ugr_results entries with _source=cie_117_default (N, S, E, W wall-facing per [ugr-rules#default-observer-positions], positions adapted to 8×6 m room). No engineer overrides supplied.
ies_files[0]._source length=298 chars ≥ 40 minimum; verification_status=synthetic_reference_C3 in enum per [ies-provenance-rules#verification-status-enum].

tool_call_pending=false; _calc_tool='calc.lumen_grid_solver'; _calc_engine_version='calc.lumen_grid_solver 1.0'. Runtime cascade completed successfully — the calc ran; the result is non-compliant by design (under-provisioned layout).
